message:
fix(fr-runtime): atomic ACL SETUSER + upstream AUTH syntax-error arity (partial br-frankenredis-faqe)

Two upstream-parity fixes in the ACL path:

1. ACL SETUSER atomicity. Upstream acl.c validates all modifiers
   against a temporary copy and only commits on full success, so
   an invalid rule leaves the user store unchanged. Our impl
   populated `entry().or_insert_with` before validating rules,
   which left a partially-initialised user behind on any parse
   error. Observable symptom: `ACL SETUSER bob invalidrule`
   created bob, making a later `ACL DELUSER bob` return 1 instead
   of 0. Refactored set_user to snapshot the prior state, hand off
   to a new apply_setuser_rules worker, and restore (or remove)
   the entry when the worker returns Err.

2. AUTH too-many-args. Upstream server.c::authCommand emits
   `shared.syntaxerr` ("ERR syntax error") when argc > 3; we
   were returning the generic "wrong number of arguments" arity
   message.

- fr-runtime: split set_user → apply_setuser_rules with rollback
- fr-runtime: AUTH returns "ERR syntax error" for argc > 3
- core_acl.json: auth_wrong_arity_too_many now expects upstream
  syntax-error; acl_deluser_cleanup_bob now expects 0
- live-oracle XFAIL list: drop auth_wrong_arity_too_many and the
  three stale DELUSER/USERS cleanup entries; the residual AUTH-
  after-SETUSER XFAILs are annotated with the harness issue
  (dedicated-runtime instances don't inherit shared-server ACL
  subsystem state)

fr-runtime 275 passed; fr-command 639 passed;
fr-conformance 180 passed, 0 failed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -9297,12 +9297,20 @@ mod tests {
                 return;
             }
         };
+        // The live-oracle harness spawns a fresh Runtime for every
+        // AUTH/HELLO case (`live_oracle_case_uses_dedicated_connection`)
+        // so connection-mode mutations do not bleed between cases,
+        // but the fresh Runtime does NOT inherit the ACL subsystem
+        // state that earlier `ACL SETUSER` cases built on the shared
+        // runtime. Upstream runs against the same persistent server
+        // from a fresh TCP connection, so its ACL subsystem survives.
+        // Every AUTH-after-SETUSER case therefore sees our fresh
+        // runtime's nopass-locked default and returns the
+        // "no password configured" error instead of matching upstream.
+        // The proper fix is to share `ServerState` (ACL + keyspace)
+        // across isolated sessions in the harness; tracked on faqe.
+        // (br-frankenredis-faqe)
         const XFAIL: &[&str] = &[
-            // AUTH against SETUSER-configured passwords: fr-runtime's
-            // default ACL state treats default as nopass-locked, so
-            // every AUTH-after-SETUSER case returns our lockout error
-            // instead of matching upstream. Tracked on faqe as the
-            // substantive AUTH-flow fix.
             "acl_category_auth_reader",
             "acl_category_reauth_default2",
             "acl_deny_override_auth",
@@ -9313,7 +9321,6 @@ mod tests {
             "auth_correct_user_pass",
             "auth_disabled_user_rejected",
             "auth_nonexistent_user",
-            "auth_wrong_arity_too_many",
             "auth_wrong_password",
         ];
         let stable: Vec<String> = fixture
